hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, each property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s.

context.street_character: One-way EB | 40 km/h | Sub-standard sidewalks | Mixed commercial-residential

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: red brick with white and green painted trim, including green zig-zag detail bands in the gable and around windows
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Decorative trim appears maintained and distinctive; the facade reads as occupied and cared for.
photo_observations.window_details: Large lower windows and smaller upper windows are framed by painted trim bands and decorative brick reveals.
photo_observations.door_details: Entry sits under a shallow porch roof with white posts and is flanked by a wrought-iron fence in the front yard.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Highly visible painted vergeboards and trim in the gable are the main decorative features.
photo_observations.bay_window_details: Shallow bay reads on the lower facade under the front gable.
photo_observations.porch_details: Compact open porch with steps and simple white columns.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Painted gable ornament, decorative window surrounds, and wrought-iron front fence emphasize the Victorian character.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Replacement windows are present, but the decorative trim remains a prominent heritage feature.
photo_observations.notes: Best frontal view of the green-trimmed bay-and-gable house.

notes: Best frontal view of the green-trimmed bay-and-gable house.

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its house-form, row typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include decorative brick, original windows, cornice.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, each property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s.

facade_description: Row house-form (1904-1913) in red brick with distinctive white and green painted trim, including green zig-zag detail bands in the gable and around windows. Two storeys under a cross-gable with a three-sided projecting bay, open porch, and decorative cornice. Subdivided into 3 dwelling units. Party walls on both sides.
